name: aDoctor
description: A tool for detecting energy code smells in Android applications.

name: aether
description: A open telemetry exporter to calculate the carbon emissions generated by your cloud infrastructure.

name: AI Power Meter
description: The library enables to monitor energy usage of machine learning programs. It uses RAPL for the CPU and nvidia-smi for the GPU.

name: Android Power Profiler
description: A tool for profiling the power consumption of Android applications.

name: Android Runner
description: Android Runner (AR) is a tool for automatically executing measurement-based experiments on native and web apps running on Android devices.

name: AWS Customer Carbon Footprint Tool
description: The AWS Customer Carbon Footprint Tool provides estimates of the carbon emissions associated with AWS products and services. Data is updated with a delay of three months.

name: Azure Cloud for Sustainability API
description: Microsoft Cloud for Sustainability provides APIs to access emissions data related to your Azure and Microsoft 365 usage.

name: Azure Emission Impact Dashboard
description: Power BI App that provides emissions for azure cloud resource usage.

name: Boavizta Cloud-Scanner for AWS
description: Boavizta Cloud-Scanner calculates environmental impacts of your cloud compute and storage instances. It combines real time inventory and usage data from your cloud account with Boavizta API to offer a view of your impacts on a given region.

name: BoaviztAPI
description: An open source API that provides environmental impact data for IT equipment, including carbon footprint, energy consumption, and resource depletion.

name: Camunda Carbon Reductor
description: A tool for optimizing business processes to reduce carbon emissions. It supports Camunda 7 and Camunda 8.

name: Carbon Aware KEDA Operator
description: A Kubernetes operator that scales workloads based on carbon intensity data.

name: Carbon Aware SDK
description: A software development kit for building carbon-aware applications that can shift workloads to times and regions with cleaner electricity.

name: Carbon-Aware-Computing.com
description: An API service and tool suite for carbon-aware computing that uses data from Energy-Charts.

name: Carbon-Intensity UK
description: An API that provides carbon intensity data for the UK electricity grid.

name: CarbonDB
description: CarbonDB collects and consolidates energy usage data from multiple sources within a company, providing a unified view of energy consumption. It accurately converts energy usage into carbon emissions, enabling precise tracking of a company's carbon footprint.

name: Carbontracker
description: A tool for tracking and predicting the energy consumption and carbon footprint of training deep learning models.

name: Cardamon
description: Cardamon is a tool to help development teams measure the power consumption and carbon emissions of their software. The tool is built around the concept of observations and scenarios.

name: CAST
description: A proprietary software intelligence platform that includes analysis of energy efficiency and carbon footprint.

name: CLEVER
description: Container Level Energy-efficient VPA Recommender for Kubernetes

name: Climatiq
description: A proprietary API that provides carbon emissions data for various activities, including cloud computing, to help organizations measure and reduce their carbon footprint.

name: Cloud Carbon Footprint
description: An open source tool that provides visibility and tooling to measure, monitor, and reduce your cloud carbon emissions.

name: Cloud Energy
description: Cloud Energy is an XGBoost & linear model based on the energy data from the SPECPower database for the cloud to estimate wattage consumption of server by just a few input variables.

name: CO2.js
description: A JavaScript library for estimating the carbon emissions of websites and web applications.

name: CodeCarbon
description: A Python package for estimating and tracking the carbon emissions of compute-intensive code.

name: Coppers
description: Coppers is a test harness for Rust that can measure the evolution of power consumptions of a Rust program between different versions with the help of Intel RAPL.

name: CPU Energy Meter
description: CPU Energy Meter is a Linux tool that allows to monitor power consumption of Intel CPUs at fine time granularities.

name: Creedengo
description: A static code analysis tool for identifying energy inefficiencies in code.

name: Cycloid
description: A platform for managing and optimizing cloud infrastructure with sustainability in mind.

name: DailyClean
description: Shutting down pods by cronjob.

name: DotNet-Counters
description: A performance monitoring tool for .NET applications that includes energy consumption metrics.

name: Dynatrace Carbon Impact
description: A proprietary solution that provides insights into the carbon footprint of cloud applications and infrastructure, helping organizations reduce their environmental impact.

name: ec0lint
description: ec0lint is a static code analysis tool that provides users with hints on how to reduce the carbon footprint of their websites during the development process.

name: Eco CI
description: Eco CI is a project aimed at estimating energy consumption in continuous integration (CI) environments. It provides functionality to calculate the energy consumption of CI jobs based on the power consumption characteristics of the underlying hardware.

name: Ecograder
description: Ecograder analyzes websites for environmental impact by evaluating performance, design, development practices, and hosting, offering insights and recommendations to improve digital sustainability. A badge is provided that can be added to the footer of websites.

name: EcoIndex
description: EcoIndex evaluates the environmental impact of web pages by analyzing technical performance, complexity, and resource usage, providing a sustainability score along with actionable recommendations for optimization.

name: EcoLogits
description: EcoLogits tracks the energy consumption and environmental impacts of using generative AI models through APIs.

name: Ecometer
description: Ecometer is a tool that assesses the environmental impact of websites by analyzing their technical structure, performance, and hosting, offering a clear eco-score and suggestions to reduce digital carbon emissions.

name: Elastic Universal Profiling
description: A profiling solution that helps identify energy-intensive code paths and optimize application performance and energy efficiency.

name: Electricity Maps
description: An API that provides real-time carbon intensity data for electricity grids around the world.

name: Energy-Charts
description: A service that provides data on electricity generation, consumption, and carbon intensity for various countries in Europe.

name: Firefox Power Profiler
description: A tool for profiling the power consumption of websites in Firefox.

name: fruggr
description: A platform for managing IT inventory and analyzing its environmental impact.

name: Goldilocks
description: A tool for identifying and setting resource requests and limits for Kubernetes workloads.

name: Google Carbon Footprint
description: The Carbon Footprint dashboard displays estimated greenhouse gas emissions associated with the usage of covered Google Cloud services for the selected billing account.

name: Green Algorithms Calculator
description: A tool for estimating the carbon footprint of computational tasks, including AI Model Training and inference.

name: Green Coding AI
description: This project aims to show how much asking an GPT uses in resources.

name: Green Cost Explorer for AWS
description: The Green Cost Explorer is a tool by The Green Web Foundation that helps visualize AWS cloud spending alongside carbon emissions data to support more sustainable cloud infrastructure decisions.

name: Green Metrics Tool
description: The Green Metrics Tool is a developer tool indented for measuring the energy and CO2 consumption of software through a software life cycle analysis (SLCA).

name: GreenFrame
description: Estimates the carbon footprint of a user scenario on a web application. Full-stack analysis (browser, screen, network, server). Can be used standalone, in a CI/CD pipeline, and in conjunction with the greenframe.io service.

name: GreenIT-Analysis
description: A browser extension for analyzing the websites for best practices.

name: Greenpixie GPX Data
description: A solution for optimizing cloud infrastructure to reduce carbon emissions.

name: Greenspector Studio
description: A proprietary solution for measuring the energy efficiency and carbon footprint of websites and mobile applications on real devices on the test bench of Greenspector.

name: Grid-aware Websites
description: A library for making websites carbon-aware by adapting to the carbon intensity of the electricity grid.

name: grid-intensity-go
description: A command-line tool, go library and Prometheus exporter for accessing carbon intensity data from various sources.

name: IBM Turbonomic
description: A platform for optimizing cloud resources to reduce costs and carbon emissions.

name: Impact Framework
description: A framework for modeling and analyzing the environmental impact of software systems.

name: InfluxData Telegraf
description: A plugin-driven server agent for collecting and reporting metrics, including energy consumption and carbon emissions.

name: Intel PCM
description: A tool for monitoring performance and energy metrics for Intel processors.

name: JoularJX
description: JoularJX is a Java-based agent for software power monitoring at the source code level.

name: jPowerMonitor
description: JUnit Extension and Java Agent for energy consumption measurement.

name: Karpenter
description: A Kubernetes node autoscaler that helps improve application availability and cluster efficiency.

name: Kastor
description: Kastor estimates the carbon emissions of a website and analyzes it according to eco design and accessibility guidelines.

name: KEDA
description: A Kubernetes-based event-driven autoscaler that can scale applications based on event metrics from various sources.

name: KEIT
description: KEIT (Kubernetes Emissions Insights Tool) supplements the energy recording with Kepler with current grid carbon intensity values and embodied carbon emissions.

name: Kepler
description: Kepler (Kubernetes-based Efficient Power Level Exporter) uses eBPF to probe performance counters and other system stats, use ML models to estimate workload energy consumption based on these stats, and exports them as Prometheus metrics.

name: kube-green
description: A Kubernetes operator to reduce CO2 footprint by shutting down idle resources.

name: Li10 for AWS
description: Measures the emissions for AWS resources with Software Carbon Intensity methodology defined by the Green Software Foundation.

name: LIMo
description: A lightweight monitoring tool for calculating the energy consumption of microservices in Docker containers.

name: ML CO2 Impact
description: A tool for estimating the carbon footprint of machine learning computations.

name: NVIDIA SMI
description: A tool for monitoring and managing NVIDIA GPUs, including power consumption.

name: oaklean
description: A tool for analyzing and optimizing the energy efficiency of Node.js applications.

name: OpenTelemetry eBPF Profiler
description: A whole-system, cross-language profiler for Linux via eBPF.

name: OpenTelemetry Java Agent Extension (RETIT)
description: An extension for the OpenTelemetry Java Agent that collects resource demand data and publishes carbon emissions.

name: OxygenIT
description: A solution for optimizing IT infrastructure to reduce energy consumption and carbon emissions.

name: Perf
description: A performance analysis tool for Linux that can also be used to measure energy consumption by using RAPL.

name: PowDroid
description: An open-source tool for measuring the power consumption of Android applications.

name: Power Hog
description: A tool for monitoring the power consumption of desktop applications.

name: powerletrics
description: PowerLetrics is an open-source framework designed to monitor and analyze power consumption metrics at the process level on Linux. Similar to how top displays memory or CPU time, PowerLetrics provides the "Energy Footprint" of each process in real-time.

name: Powerstat
description: Powerstat measures the power consumption of a machine using the battery stats or the Intel RAPL interface. The output is like vmstat but also shows power consumption statistics.

name: PowerTOP
description: A tool for diagnosing issues with power consumption and power management on Linux.

name: PyRAPL
description: A Python toolkit for measuring the energy consumption of Python code using Intel's Running Average Power Limit (RAPL).

name: ResilioDB
description: ResilioDB breaks down the electronic components of a device like RAM or CPU, in order to assess the environmental impacts of any possible configuration.

name: Safari Web Inspector
description: A tool for inspecting and profiling websites in Safari, including energy impact.

name: Scaleway Environment Footprint Calculator
description: Scaleway's Environmental footprint calculator allows you to measure your environmental impact to your use of Scaleway cloud and bare metal services.

name: Scaphandre
description: A monitoring agent dedicated to energy consumption metrics of bare metal hosts and qemu/kvm virtual machines.

name: Tracarbon
description: Tracarbon is a Python library that tracks your device's energy consumption and calculates your carbon emissions.

name: Turbostat
description: A tool for reporting processor topology, frequency, idle power-state statistics, temperature and power on X86 processors.

name: txture
description: A platform for analyzing and optimizing cloud infrastructure for cost and sustainability.

name: Watt Wiser
description: A desktop software energy consumption estimation tool built as part of the [Watt-Wise Game Jam](https://wattwise.games/). Usable for simple energy estimation and benchmarking on Linux, macOS, and Windows.

name: WattTime
description: An API that provides real-time and forecast marginal carbon emissions data for electricity grids.

name: Website Carbon Calculator
description: Website Carbon Calculator estimates the carbon emissions of a website by analyzing factors like data transfer size and energy source of the hosting provider. A badge is provided that can be added to the footer of websites.

name: ZEUS
description: Zeus is a library for measuring the energy consumption of Deep Learning workloads and optimizing their energy consumption.
